```js
// Full path: /home/luanngo/boss-dev/server/src/routes/api/accounting/payroll-schedules.js
// server/src/routes/api/accounting/payroll-schedules.js
import express from "express";
import authenticate from "#middleware/auth.js";
import PayrollService from "#services/accounting/services/PayrollService.js";

const router = express.Router();
const payrollService = new PayrollService();

// GET all payroll schedules for the organization.
router.get("/", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const schedules = await payrollService.getPayrollSchedules(organizationId);
    return res.status(200).json(schedules);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Payroll schedules GET error:", error.message);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Failed to fetch payroll schedules." });
  }
});

// POST generate a new payroll schedule.
// Expects JSON body: { startWeek: 'YYYY-MM-DD', endWeek: 'YYYY-MM-DD', status: 'Active', notes: 'Optional notes' }
router.post("/generate", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const { startWeek, endWeek, status, notes } = req.body;
    if (!startWeek || !endWeek) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing required fields: startWeek, endWeek." });
    }
    const schedule = await payrollService.generatePayrollSchedule(
      organizationId,
      startWeek,
      endWeek,
      status || "Active",
      notes || ""
    );
    return res.status(200).json(schedule);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Generate payroll schedule error:", error.message);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Failed to generate payroll schedule." });
  }
});

// DELETE a payroll schedule (cascade deletes payroll records).
router.delete("/:scheduleId", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const { scheduleId } = req.params;
    if (!scheduleId) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing scheduleId parameter." });
    }
    await payrollService.deletePayrollSchedule(organizationId, scheduleId);
    return res.status(200).json({ message: "Payroll schedule deleted successfully." });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Delete payroll schedule error:", error.message);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Failed to delete payroll schedule." });
  }
});

export default router;

```

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FILE: server/src/routes/api/accounting/payroll.js
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```js
// Full path: /home/luanngo/boss-dev/server/src/routes/api/accounting/payroll.js
import express from "express";
import authenticate from "#middleware/auth.js";
import PayrollService from "#services/accounting/services/PayrollService.js";
import { addDays, format, parseISO, isValid as isDateValid } from "date-fns"; // Added isValid
import prisma from "#shared/database/prismaClient.js";
import moment from "moment-timezone";

const router = express.Router();
const payrollService = new PayrollService();
router.get("/", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const { scheduleId } = req.query;

    if (!scheduleId) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing scheduleId parameter." });
    }

    const schedule = await prisma.payrollSchedule.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: scheduleId,
        organizationId,
      },
      select: { id: true, startWeek: true, endWeek: true }
    });

    if (!schedule) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Payroll schedule not found or access denied." });
    }

    const payrollRecords = await prisma.payrollRecord.findMany({
      where: {
        payrollScheduleId: scheduleId,
        user: {
          organizationId,
          OR: [
            { active: true },
            { AND: [{ active: false }, { pay: { gt: 0 } }] } // include inactive users with a pay value
          ]
        }
      },
      include: {
        user: {
          select: { id: true, firstName: true, lastName: true, username: true }
        },
        adjustments: {
          orderBy: { date: 'asc' }
        }
      },
      orderBy: {
        user: { lastName: 'asc' }
      }
    });

    return res.status(200).json(payrollRecords);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Payroll GET (by scheduleId) Error:", error.message, error.stack);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error fetching payroll records." });
  }
});

router.get("/by-range", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const { start, end } = req.query;

    if (!start || !end) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing start or end date parameters." });
    }

    const payrollRecords = await prisma.payrollRecord.findMany({
      where: {
        payrollSchedule: {
          organizationId,
          OR: [
            {
              startWeek: { gte: new Date(start), lte: new Date(end) }
            },
            {
              endWeek: { gte: new Date(start), lte: new Date(end) }
            },
            {
              AND: [
                { startWeek: { lte: new Date(start) } },
                { endWeek: { gte: new Date(end) } }
              ]
            }
          ]
        },
        user: {
          organizationId,
          OR: [
            { active: true },
            { AND: [{ active: false }, { pay: { gt: 0 } }] }
          ]
        }
      },
      include: {
        user: {
          select: { id: true, firstName: true, lastName: true, username: true }
        },
        adjustments: {
          orderBy: { date: 'asc' }
        }
      },
      orderBy: {
        user: { lastName: 'asc' }
      }
    });

    return res.status(200).json(payrollRecords);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Payroll GET (by range) Error:", error.message, error.stack);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error fetching payroll records by range." });
  }
});

router.get("/generate", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const { scheduleId, weekStart, dayStart, dayEnd } = req.query;
    let periodStart, periodEnd;
    if (dayStart && dayEnd) {
      periodStart = dayStart;
      periodEnd = dayEnd;
    } else {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing scheduleId, weekStart, or dayStart/dayEnd parameters." });
    }

    const data = await payrollService.genPayrollData(organizationId, periodStart, periodEnd);
    return res.status(200).json(data);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Payroll GET Error:", error.message);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error." });
  }
});

// Update (or create) a payroll record.
router.post("/update", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const {
      userId,
      payrollScheduleId, // required for linking records
      hourlyWage,
      allowableHours,
      bonus,
      tips,
      notes,
      scheduledHours, // new (optional)
      actualHours,     // new (optional)
      visible
    } = req.body;
    if (!userId || !payrollScheduleId || hourlyWage === undefined || allowableHours === undefined) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing required fields." });
    }
    const record = await payrollService.updatePayrollRecord({
      organizationId,
      userId,
      payrollScheduleId,
      hourlyWage: parseFloat(hourlyWage),
      allowableHours: parseFloat(allowableHours),
      bonus: parseFloat(bonus || 0),
      tips: parseFloat(tips || 0),
      notes,
      scheduledHours: scheduledHours !== undefined ? parseFloat(scheduledHours) : undefined,
      actualHours: actualHours !== undefined ? parseFloat(actualHours) : undefined,
      visible
    });
    return res.status(200).json(record);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Payroll UPDATE Error:", error.message);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error." });
  }
});

router.get("/tips-breakdown", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    // Primarily expect startTime and endTime (as UNIX seconds)
    const { scheduleId, weekStart, dayStart, dayEnd, startTime, endTime } = req.query;

    let startTimestampSec, endTimestampSec;

    if (startTime && endTime && !isNaN(startTime) && !isNaN(endTime)) {
      startTimestampSec = parseInt(startTime, 10);
      endTimestampSec = parseInt(endTime, 10);
    } else {
       // Fallback logic if startTime/endTime aren't provided (less preferred)
       let periodStart, periodEnd;
       if (dayStart && dayEnd) {
         periodStart = moment.tz(dayStart, 'YYYY-MM-DD', "America/New_York").startOf('day').toDate();
         // dayEnd is inclusive, so get the end of that day
         periodEnd = moment.tz(dayEnd, 'YYYY-MM-DD', "America/New_York").endOf('day').toDate();
       } else if (scheduleId) {
         const schedule = await payrollService.getPayrollSchedule(organizationId, scheduleId);
         if (!schedule) return res.status(404).json({ error: "Payroll schedule not found." });
         periodStart = schedule.startWeek;
         periodEnd = schedule.endWeek;
       } else if (weekStart) {
         periodStart = moment.tz(weekStart, 'YYYY-MM-DD', "America/New_York").startOf('day').toDate();
         periodEnd = moment(periodStart).add(6, 'days').endOf('day').toDate();
       } else {
         return res.status(400).json({ error: "Missing required time parameters (startTime/endTime preferred)." });
       }
       startTimestampSec = Math.floor(periodStart.getTime() / 1000);
       endTimestampSec = Math.floor(moment(periodEnd).startOf('day').toDate().getTime() / 1000);
    }

    if (startTimestampSec === undefined || endTimestampSec === undefined) {
       return res.status(400).json({ error: "Could not determine valid start and end timestamps." });
    }

    const breakdownData = await payrollService.getTipBreakdownForWeek(
      organizationId,
      startTimestampSec,
      endTimestampSec
    );
    return res.status(200).json(breakdownData);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Tips Breakdown GET Error:", error.message, error.stack);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error fetching tips breakdown." });
  }
});

router.get("/tips-detail/:date", authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
      const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
      const { date } = req.params;

      // Validate date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
      if (!/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/.test(date) || !isDateValid(parseISO(date))) {
           return res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid date format. Please use YYYY-MM-DD." });
      }

      const detailedData = await payrollService.getDetailedTipBreakdownForDay(organizationId, date);
      return res.status(200).json(detailedData);
  } catch (error) {
      console.error(`Detailed Tips GET Error for date ${req.params.date}:`, error.message, error.stack);
      return res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error fetching detailed tip breakdown." });
  }
});

export default router;

```

// Full path: /home/luanngo/boss-dev/server/src/routes/api/accounting/reviewQueue.js
// Full path: /home/luan_ngo/boss/server/src/routes/api/accounting/reviewQueue.js
import express from 'express';
import authenticate from '#middleware/auth.js';
import AccountingService from '#services/accounting/services/AccountingService.js';
import AccountingLLMService from '#services/accounting/services/AccountingLLMService.js';

const router = express.Router();
const accountingService = new AccountingService();

const findSimilarUncategorizedTransactions = async ({ organizationId, transactionId, transaction, proposal = null }) => {
  return accountingService.findSimilarTransactionsForProposal(
    organizationId,
    { ...transaction, id: Number(transactionId) },
    proposal || {},
    { uncategorizedOnly: true, limit: 250 }
  );
};


const normalizeRulePatternList = (value) => {
  if (!Array.isArray(value)) return [];
  return Array.from(new Set(value.map((item) => String(item || '').trim()).filter(Boolean)));
};


const inferTaxTreatmentFromTransaction = (transaction, postingType = null) => {
  const normalizedPostingType = String(postingType || transaction?.postingType || '').trim().toUpperCase();
  if ([
    'PAYROLL_FUNDING',
    'PAYROLL_PAYMENT',
    'PAYROLL_REMITTANCE',
    'TAX_REMITTANCE',
    'TRANSFER',
    'CREDIT_CARD_PAYMENT',
    'PAYMENT_PROCESSOR_PAYOUT',
    'PAYMENT_PROCESSOR_REVERSAL',
    'OWNER_DRAW',
    'OWNER_CONTRIBUTION',
  ].includes(normalizedPostingType)) {
    return 'NO_TAX';
  }
  const taxValue = Number(transaction?.taxes || 0);
  if (taxValue <= 0) return 'NO_TAX';
  return Number(transaction?.amount || 0) > 0 ? 'SALES_TAX_PAYABLE' : 'INPUT_TAX_CREDIT';
};

const serializeRuleMeta = (meta = {}) => JSON.stringify({
  patterns: normalizeRulePatternList(meta.patterns),
  group: String(meta.group || 'General').trim() || 'General',
  notes: String(meta.notes || '').trim(),
  enabled: meta.enabled !== false,
  matchMode: String(meta.matchMode || 'ANY').toUpperCase() === 'ALL' ? 'ALL' : 'ANY',
  sourceType: String(meta.sourceType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  transactionType: String(meta.transactionType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  taxTreatment: String(meta.taxTreatment || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  amountDirection: ['ANY', 'EXPENSE', 'INCOME'].includes(String(meta.amountDirection || '').toUpperCase())
    ? String(meta.amountDirection).toUpperCase()
    : 'ANY',
  llmSummary: String(meta.llmSummary || '').trim(),
  proposalSource: String(meta.proposalSource || '').trim() || null,
  journalMode: String(meta.journalMode || 'STANDARD').toUpperCase() === 'PROPOSAL_HINT' ? 'PROPOSAL_HINT' : 'STANDARD',
  journalTemplateLines: Array.isArray(meta.journalTemplateLines) ? meta.journalTemplateLines : [],
});

const resolveRuleDraftContext = async ({ organizationId, transactionId, userId, sessionId, overrides = {} }) => {
  const transaction = await accountingService.prisma.bankTransaction.findFirst({
    where: { id: transactionId, organizationId },
    include: {
      category: { select: { id: true, code: true, description: true, statementLine: true } },
      receipt: true,
      source: { select: { id: true, name: true, type: true } },
    },
  });

  if (!transaction) {
    const error = new Error('Transaction not found.');
    error.statusCode = 404;
    throw error;
  }

  let overrideAccount = null;
  if (overrides.accountId) {
    overrideAccount = await accountingService.prisma.accountingCategory.findUnique({ where: { id: overrides.accountId } });
  } else if (overrides.accountCode) {
    overrideAccount = await accountingService.prisma.accountingCategory.findUnique({ where: { code: overrides.accountCode } });
  }

  const hydrated = accountingService.getAIProposalFromNotes(transaction.notes) || {};
  const effectiveTransactionType = overrides.transactionType || hydrated?.likelyTransactionType || transaction.postingType || null;
  const normalizedProposal = {
    ...hydrated,
    likelyAccount: overrideAccount || hydrated?.likelyAccount || transaction.category || null,
    likelyTransactionType: effectiveTransactionType,
    likelyTaxTreatment: overrides.taxTreatment || hydrated?.likelyTaxTreatment || inferTaxTreatmentFromTransaction(transaction, effectiveTransactionType),
    likelyJournalLines: Array.isArray(overrides.journalLines) && overrides.journalLines.length
      ? overrides.journalLines
      : Array.isArray(hydrated?.likelyJournalLines)
        ? hydrated.likelyJournalLines
        : [],
  };

  const similarTransactions = await accountingService.findSimilarTransactionsForProposal(
    organizationId,
    transaction,
    normalizedProposal,
    { uncategorizedOnly: true, limit: 250 }
  );

  const draft = await AccountingLLMService.draftRuleFromProposal({
    organizationId,
    userId,
    sessionId,
    transaction,
    proposal: normalizedProposal,
    similarTransactions,
  });

  return {
    transaction,
    normalizedProposal,
    similarTransactions,
    draft,
  };
};

const saveDraftAsRule = async ({ organizationId, draft = {} }) => {
  const patternsPayload = serializeRuleMeta({
    patterns: Array.isArray(draft.patterns) ? draft.patterns : [],
    group: draft.group || 'General',
    notes: draft.notes || '',
    enabled: draft.enabled !== false,
    matchMode: draft.matchMode || 'ANY',
    sourceType: draft.sourceType || null,
    transactionType: draft.transactionType || null,
    taxTreatment: draft.taxTreatment || null,
    amountDirection: draft.amountDirection || 'ANY',
    llmSummary: draft.llmSummary || '',
    proposalSource: draft.proposalSource || 'REVIEW_QUEUE',
    journalMode: draft.journalMode || 'STANDARD',
    journalTemplateLines: Array.isArray(draft.journalTemplateLines) ? draft.journalTemplateLines : [],
  });

  return accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.create({
    data: {
      organizationId,
      name: draft.name,
      sql: draft.sql || '',
      patterns: patternsPayload,
      autoCategory: Boolean(draft.autoCategory && draft.categoryId),
      autoApplyTaxes: Boolean(draft.autoApplyTaxes),
      requiredReceipt: Boolean(draft.requiredReceipt),
      categoryId: draft.categoryId || null,
    },
    include: { category: true },
  });
};

router.get('/', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const limit = Number(req.query.limit || 25);

    const items = await accountingService.getReviewQueue(
      organizationId,
      { limit }
    );

    return res.status(200).json({ items });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to load review queue.' });
  }
});

router.post('/propose-selected', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;
    const transactionIds = Array.isArray(req.body?.transactionIds) ? req.body.transactionIds : [];

    const items = await accountingService.generateReviewQueueProposals(
      organizationId,
      transactionIds,
      { userId, sessionId }
    );

    return res.status(200).json({
      message: 'Proposals generated for selected transactions.',
      items,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Propose Selected Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to generate proposals for selected transactions.' });
  }
});

router.post('/:transactionId/propose', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;

    const items = await accountingService.generateReviewQueueProposals(
      organizationId,
      [req.params.transactionId],
      { userId, sessionId }
    );

    const item = items[0] || null;
    if (!item) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Transaction not found.' });
    }

    return res.status(200).json({ item });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Propose Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to generate review proposal.' });
  }
});

router.post('/:transactionId/approve', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const transactionId = req.params.transactionId;
    const payload = req.body || {};

    const baseTransaction = await accountingService.prisma.bankTransaction.findFirst({
      where: { id: Number(transactionId), organizationId },
      include: { category: true, receipt: true, source: true, journalEntry: true },
    });

    if (!baseTransaction) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Transaction not found.' });
    }

    const updatedTransaction = await accountingService.approveReviewQueueItem(
      organizationId,
      transactionId,
      payload,
      { userId }
    );

    let similarTransactionsUpdated = 0;
    const similarTransactionIds = [];

    if (payload.applyToSimilar) {
      let targetIds = Array.isArray(payload.targetTransactionIds)
        ? payload.targetTransactionIds.map((value) => Number(value)).filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value))
        : [];

      if (!targetIds.length) {
        const similarTransactions = await findSimilarUncategorizedTransactions({
          organizationId,
          transactionId,
          transaction: baseTransaction,
          proposal: updatedTransaction?.aiProposal || payload.proposal || null,
        });
        targetIds = similarTransactions.map((item) => Number(item.id)).filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value));
      }

      for (const similarTransactionId of targetIds) {
        await accountingService.approveReviewQueueItem(
          organizationId,
          similarTransactionId,
          {
            accountId: payload.accountId || null,
            accountCode: payload.accountCode || null,
            transactionType: payload.transactionType || null,
            taxTreatment: payload.taxTreatment || null,
            journalLines: Array.isArray(payload.journalLines) ? payload.journalLines : null,
          },
          { userId }
        );
        similarTransactionsUpdated += 1;
        similarTransactionIds.push(similarTransactionId);
      }
    }

    return res.status(200).json({
      message: 'Proposal accepted and journal synced.',
      transaction: updatedTransaction,
      similarTransactionsUpdated,
      similarTransactionIds,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Approve Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to approve proposal.' });
  }
});

router.post('/:transactionId/create-rule-draft', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;
    const transactionId = Number(req.params.transactionId);

    const { draft, similarTransactions } = await resolveRuleDraftContext({
      organizationId,
      transactionId,
      userId,
      sessionId,
      overrides: req.body || {},
    });

    return res.status(200).json({
      message: 'Rule suggestion ready for approval.',
      draft,
      matchedTransactionCount: similarTransactions.length + 1,
      sampleTransactionIds: [transactionId, ...similarTransactions.slice(0, 9).map((item) => item.id)],
      sampleTransactions: similarTransactions.slice(0, 8).map((item) => ({
        id: item.id,
        date: item.date,
        description: item.description,
        amount: item.amount,
      })),
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Create Rule Draft Error:', error);
    return res.status(error.statusCode || 500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to draft rule from proposal.' });
  }
});

router.post('/:transactionId/create-rule/approve', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const transactionId = Number(req.params.transactionId);
    const draft = req.body?.draft || null;

    if (!draft || typeof draft !== 'object') {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'A rule draft is required before saving.' });
    }
    if (!String(draft.name || '').trim()) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Draft name is required.' });
    }

    const createdRule = await saveDraftAsRule({ organizationId, draft: { ...draft, proposalSource: draft.proposalSource || 'REVIEW_QUEUE' } });

    return res.status(201).json({
      message: 'Rule approved and saved.',
      rule: createdRule,
      transactionId,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Approve Rule Draft Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to save approved rule draft.' });
  }
});

router.post('/:transactionId/reject', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const updatedTransaction = await accountingService.rejectReviewQueueItem(
      organizationId,
      req.params.transactionId,
      req.body || {}
    );

    return res.status(200).json({
      message: 'Proposal dismissed.',
      transaction: updatedTransaction,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Review Queue Reject Error:', error);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: error.message || 'Failed to reject proposal.' });
  }
});

export default router;

FILE: server/src/routes/api/accounting/rules.js

```js
// Full path: /home/luanngo/boss-dev/server/src/routes/api/accounting/rules.js
import express from "express";
import authenticate from "#middleware/auth.js";
import AccountingService from "#services/accounting/services/AccountingService.js";
import AccountingLLMService from "#services/accounting/services/AccountingLLMService.js";
import RuleSuggestionService from "#services/accounting/services/RuleSuggestionService.js";
import TransactionGroupService from "#services/accounting/services/TransactionGroupService.js";
import { withSqliteMutex } from "../../../utils/sqliteMutex.js";
import { getIO } from "../../../shared/socket.js";

const router = express.Router();
const accountingService = new AccountingService();
const RULE_DELAY_MS = Number(process.env.ACCOUNTING_RULE_DELAY_MS || 1000);

const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
const isLockOrTimeout = (err) => {
  const msg = String(err?.message || "").toLowerCase();
  return (
    err?.code === "P1008" ||
    msg.includes("database is locked") ||
    msg.includes("database busy") ||
    msg.includes("locking") ||
    msg.includes("timeout")
  );
};

function scopeSqlToIds(sqlRaw, ids) {
  if (!ids || ids.length === 0) return null;

  let sql = String(sqlRaw || "").trim().split(';')[0];
  if (!sql) return null;

  if (/\{\{IDS\}\}/.test(sql)) {
    return `${sql.replace(/\{\{IDS\}\}/g, ids.join(","))};`;
  }

  const idClause = `\"BankTransaction\".\"id\" IN (${ids.join(",")})`;
  const lower = sql.toLowerCase();
  const whereIdx = lower.lastIndexOf(" where ");

  if (whereIdx !== -1) {
    sql = `${sql} AND ${idClause}`;
  } else {
    sql = `${sql} WHERE ${idClause}`;
  }

  return `${sql};`;
}

const normalizePatternList = (value) => {
  if (!Array.isArray(value)) return [];
  return Array.from(new Set(value.map((item) => String(item || '').trim()).filter(Boolean)));
};

const normalizePreviousTaxRate = (value) => {
  if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') return null;
  const ratePercent = Number(value.ratePercent);
  if (!Number.isFinite(ratePercent) || ratePercent < 0) return null;
  const inclusiveRate = Number.isFinite(Number(value.inclusiveRate))
    ? Number(value.inclusiveRate)
    : ratePercent / 100;
  const taxShareOfGross = Number.isFinite(Number(value.taxShareOfGross))
    ? Number(value.taxShareOfGross)
    : inclusiveRate / (1 + inclusiveRate);

  return {
    source: value.source || 'manual_edit',
    sampleCount: Number(value.sampleCount || 0),
    inclusiveRate: Number(inclusiveRate.toFixed(6)),
    taxShareOfGross: Number(taxShareOfGross.toFixed(6)),
    ratePercent: Number(ratePercent.toFixed(2)),
  };
};

const parseRuleMeta = (rawValue) => {
  const defaults = {
    patterns: [],
    group: 'General',
    notes: '',
    enabled: true,
    matchMode: 'ANY',
    sourceType: null,
    transactionType: null,
    taxTreatment: null,
    amountDirection: 'ANY',
    llmSummary: '',
    proposalSource: null,
    journalMode: 'STANDARD',
    journalTemplateLines: [],
    previousTaxRate: null,
    applyPreviousTaxRate: false,
  };

  if (!rawValue) return defaults;

  try {
    const parsed = typeof rawValue === 'string' ? JSON.parse(rawValue) : rawValue;
    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
      return { ...defaults, patterns: normalizePatternList(parsed) };
    }
    if (parsed && typeof parsed === 'object') {
      return {
        ...defaults,
        ...parsed,
        patterns: normalizePatternList(parsed.patterns),
        group: String(parsed.group || defaults.group).trim() || defaults.group,
        notes: String(parsed.notes || '').trim(),
        enabled: parsed.enabled !== false,
        matchMode: String(parsed.matchMode || defaults.matchMode).toUpperCase() === 'ALL' ? 'ALL' : 'ANY',
        sourceType: String(parsed.sourceType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
        transactionType: String(parsed.transactionType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
        taxTreatment: String(parsed.taxTreatment || '').toUpperCase() || null,
        amountDirection: ['ANY', 'EXPENSE', 'INCOME'].includes(String(parsed.amountDirection || '').toUpperCase())
          ? String(parsed.amountDirection).toUpperCase()
          : 'ANY',
        llmSummary: String(parsed.llmSummary || '').trim(),
        proposalSource: String(parsed.proposalSource || '').trim() || null,
        journalMode: String(parsed.journalMode || defaults.journalMode).toUpperCase() === 'PROPOSAL_HINT' ? 'PROPOSAL_HINT' : 'STANDARD',
        journalTemplateLines: Array.isArray(parsed.journalTemplateLines) ? parsed.journalTemplateLines : [],
        previousTaxRate: normalizePreviousTaxRate(parsed.previousTaxRate),
        applyPreviousTaxRate: Boolean(parsed.applyPreviousTaxRate),
      };
    }
  } catch (error) {
    console.warn('Invalid accounting rule metadata:', error.message);
  }

  return defaults;
};

const serializeRuleMeta = (meta = {}) => JSON.stringify({
  patterns: normalizePatternList(meta.patterns),
  group: String(meta.group || 'General').trim() || 'General',
  notes: String(meta.notes || '').trim(),
  enabled: meta.enabled !== false,
  matchMode: String(meta.matchMode || 'ANY').toUpperCase() === 'ALL' ? 'ALL' : 'ANY',
  sourceType: String(meta.sourceType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  transactionType: String(meta.transactionType || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  taxTreatment: String(meta.taxTreatment || '').toUpperCase() || null,
  amountDirection: ['ANY', 'EXPENSE', 'INCOME'].includes(String(meta.amountDirection || '').toUpperCase())
    ? String(meta.amountDirection).toUpperCase()
    : 'ANY',
  llmSummary: String(meta.llmSummary || '').trim(),
  proposalSource: String(meta.proposalSource || '').trim() || null,
  journalMode: String(meta.journalMode || 'STANDARD').toUpperCase() === 'PROPOSAL_HINT' ? 'PROPOSAL_HINT' : 'STANDARD',
  journalTemplateLines: Array.isArray(meta.journalTemplateLines) ? meta.journalTemplateLines : [],
  previousTaxRate: normalizePreviousTaxRate(meta.previousTaxRate),
  applyPreviousTaxRate: Boolean(meta.applyPreviousTaxRate),
});

const normalizeText = (value) => String(value || '')
  .toLowerCase()
  .replace(/[^a-z0-9\s]/g, ' ')
  .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
  .trim();

const inferPatternFromRuleName = (name) => {
  const cleaned = String(name || '')
    .replace(/\s+-\s+(credit card|bank|cash)?\s*(expense|income|revenue|rule|transaction)s?.*$/i, '')
    .replace(/\s+rule$/i, '')
    .trim();
  const normalized = normalizeText(cleaned);
  return normalized.split(' ').length >= 2 ? cleaned : '';
};

const getEffectivePatterns = (meta = {}, ruleName = '') => {
  const patterns = normalizePatternList(meta?.patterns);
  if (patterns.length) return patterns;
  const inferred = inferPatternFromRuleName(ruleName);
  return inferred ? [inferred] : [];
};

const NO_TAX_POSTING_TYPES = new Set([
  'PAYROLL_FUNDING',
  'PAYROLL_PAYMENT',
  'PAYROLL_REMITTANCE',
  'TAX_REMITTANCE',
  'TRANSFER',
  'CREDIT_CARD_PAYMENT',
  'PAYMENT_PROCESSOR_PAYOUT',
  'PAYMENT_PROCESSOR_REVERSAL',
  'OWNER_DRAW',
  'OWNER_CONTRIBUTION',
]);
const isNoTaxTreatment = (value) => {
  const normalized = String(value || '').trim().toUpperCase();
  return normalized === 'NO_TAX' || normalized === 'NO_ITC' || normalized === 'EXEMPT_PURCHASE' || normalized === 'ZERO_RATED';
};

const transactionNeedsTax = (transaction, ruleMeta = {}) => {
  if (Math.abs(Number(transaction?.taxes || 0)) > 0) return false;
  if (isNoTaxTreatment(ruleMeta?.taxTreatment)) return false;

  const storedProposal = accountingService.getAIProposalFromNotes(transaction?.notes) || {};
  if (isNoTaxTreatment(storedProposal?.likelyTaxTreatment)) return false;

  const postingType = String(transaction?.postingType || storedProposal?.likelyTransactionType || ruleMeta?.transactionType || '').trim().toUpperCase();
  if (NO_TAX_POSTING_TYPES.has(postingType)) return false;

  return true;
};

const matchesPatterns = (description, meta, ruleName = '') => {
  const patterns = getEffectivePatterns(meta, ruleName);
  if (!patterns.length) return false;
  const haystack = normalizeText(description);
  if (!haystack) return false;
  const normalizedPatterns = patterns.map((pattern) => normalizeText(pattern));
  if (meta?.matchMode === 'ALL') {
    return normalizedPatterns.every((pattern) => haystack.includes(pattern));
  }
  return normalizedPatterns.some((pattern) => haystack.includes(pattern));
};

const formatRule = (rule) => {
  const meta = parseRuleMeta(rule?.patterns);
  const effectivePatterns = getEffectivePatterns(meta, rule?.name);
  return {
    ...rule,
    meta: {
      ...meta,
      patterns: effectivePatterns,
      inferredPattern: !meta.patterns.length && effectivePatterns.length > 0,
    },
    patternList: effectivePatterns,
  };
};

const buildRuleDataFromBody = (body = {}, existing = null) => {
  const existingMeta = parseRuleMeta(existing?.patterns);
  const nextMeta = {
    ...existingMeta,
    ...(body.meta && typeof body.meta === 'object' ? body.meta : {}),
    patterns: Array.isArray(body.patterns)
      ? body.patterns
      : Array.isArray(body.meta?.patterns)
        ? body.meta.patterns
        : existingMeta.patterns,
    group: body.group ?? body.meta?.group ?? existingMeta.group,
    notes: body.notes ?? body.meta?.notes ?? existingMeta.notes,
    enabled: typeof body.enabled === 'boolean' ? body.enabled : typeof body.meta?.enabled === 'boolean' ? body.meta.enabled : existingMeta.enabled,
    matchMode: body.matchMode ?? body.meta?.matchMode ?? existingMeta.matchMode,
    sourceType: body.sourceType ?? body.meta?.sourceType ?? existingMeta.sourceType,
    transactionType: body.transactionType ?? body.meta?.transactionType ?? existingMeta.transactionType,
    taxTreatment: body.taxTreatment ?? body.meta?.taxTreatment ?? existingMeta.taxTreatment,
    previousTaxRate: body.previousTaxRate ?? body.meta?.previousTaxRate ?? existingMeta.previousTaxRate,
    applyPreviousTaxRate: body.applyPreviousTaxRate ?? body.meta?.applyPreviousTaxRate ?? existingMeta.applyPreviousTaxRate,
    amountDirection: body.amountDirection ?? body.meta?.amountDirection ?? existingMeta.amountDirection,
    llmSummary: body.llmSummary ?? body.meta?.llmSummary ?? existingMeta.llmSummary,
    proposalSource: body.proposalSource ?? body.meta?.proposalSource ?? existingMeta.proposalSource,
    journalMode: body.journalMode ?? body.meta?.journalMode ?? existingMeta.journalMode,
    journalTemplateLines: Array.isArray(body.journalTemplateLines)
      ? body.journalTemplateLines
      : Array.isArray(body.meta?.journalTemplateLines)
        ? body.meta.journalTemplateLines
        : existingMeta.journalTemplateLines,
  };

  const data = {
    name: String(body.name ?? existing?.name ?? '').trim(),
    sql: String(body.sql ?? existing?.sql ?? '').trim(),
    patterns: serializeRuleMeta(nextMeta),
    autoCategory: typeof body.autoCategory === 'boolean' ? body.autoCategory : Boolean(existing?.autoCategory),
    autoApplyTaxes: typeof body.autoApplyTaxes === 'boolean' ? body.autoApplyTaxes : Boolean(existing?.autoApplyTaxes),
    requiredReceipt: typeof body.requiredReceipt === 'boolean' ? body.requiredReceipt : Boolean(existing?.requiredReceipt),
    categoryId: body.categoryId === '' ? null : body.categoryId ?? existing?.categoryId ?? null,
  };

  const status = String(body.status || '').toUpperCase();
  if (['DRAFT', 'ACTIVE', 'PAUSED', 'DEPRECATED', 'NEEDS_REVIEW'].includes(status)) {
    data.status = status;
  }

  return data;
};

async function applyRuleMetadataUpdates(prisma, rule, idsToUpdate) {
  if (!idsToUpdate.length) return 0;
  const meta = parseRuleMeta(rule.patterns);
  let changedCount = 0;

  const targets = await prisma.bankTransaction.findMany({
    where: { id: { in: idsToUpdate } },
    select: { id: true, amount: true, taxes: true, notes: true },
  });

  for (const target of targets) {
    const updateData = {};
    if (rule.autoCategory && rule.categoryId) updateData.categoryId = rule.categoryId;
    if (meta.transactionType) updateData.postingType = meta.transactionType;

    if ((rule.autoApplyTaxes || meta.applyPreviousTaxRate) && !isNoTaxTreatment(meta.taxTreatment) && Number(target.amount || 0) < 0 && (target.taxes === null || target.taxes === undefined || Number(target.taxes || 0) === 0)) {
      const absAmount = Math.abs(Number(target.amount || 0));
      const taxShareOfGross = Number(meta.previousTaxRate?.taxShareOfGross || 0) || (0.13 / 1.13);
      updateData.taxes = Number(((absAmount * taxShareOfGross) || 0).toFixed(2));
    }

    if (meta.transactionType || meta.taxTreatment || (Array.isArray(meta.journalTemplateLines) && meta.journalTemplateLines.length)) {
      updateData.notes = accountingService.mergeAIProposalIntoNotes(target.notes, {
        ...(meta.transactionType ? { likelyTransactionType: meta.transactionType } : {}),
        ...(meta.taxTreatment ? { likelyTaxTreatment: meta.taxTreatment } : {}),
        ...(Array.isArray(meta.journalTemplateLines) ? { likelyJournalLines: meta.journalTemplateLines } : {}),
      });
    }

    if (!Object.keys(updateData).length) continue;
    await prisma.bankTransaction.update({ where: { id: target.id }, data: updateData, select: { id: true } });
    changedCount += 1;
  }

  return changedCount;
}

router.post('/ai-draft', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;
    const prompt = String(req.body?.prompt || '').trim();
    const selectedIds = Array.isArray(req.body?.selectedTransactionIds)
      ? req.body.selectedTransactionIds.map((value) => Number(value)).filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value))
      : [];

    if (!prompt) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Prompt is required.' });
    }

    const selectedTransactions = selectedIds.length
      ? await accountingService.prisma.bankTransaction.findMany({
          where: { organizationId, id: { in: selectedIds } },
          include: {
            category: { select: { id: true, code: true, description: true, statementLine: true } },
            receipt: true,
            source: { select: { id: true, name: true, type: true } },
          },
          orderBy: { date: 'desc' },
          take: 25,
        })
      : [];

    const draft = await AccountingLLMService.draftRuleFromPrompt({
      organizationId,
      userId,
      sessionId,
      prompt,
      existingRule: req.body?.existingRule || null,
      selectedTransactions,
    });

    return res.json({ draft });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('AI Rule Draft Error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to draft rule.' });
  }
});


router.post('/:ruleId/ai-analyze', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const userId = req.user.id || req.user.userId;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;
    const sampleLimit = Math.min(Number(req.body?.limit || 12), 50);

    let currentRule = null;
    if (req.params.ruleId === '__DRAFT__') {
      const draftData = buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body?.rule || {});
      currentRule = formatRule({
        id: '__DRAFT__',
        name: draftData.name,
        sql: draftData.sql,
        patterns: draftData.patterns,
        autoCategory: draftData.autoCategory,
        autoApplyTaxes: draftData.autoApplyTaxes,
        requiredReceipt: draftData.requiredReceipt,
        categoryId: draftData.categoryId,
        category: draftData.categoryId
          ? await accountingService.prisma.accountingCategory.findUnique({ where: { id: draftData.categoryId } })
          : null,
      });
    } else {
      const savedRule = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: String(req.params.ruleId) }, include: { category: true } });
      if (!savedRule || savedRule.organizationId !== organizationId) {
        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found.' });
      }
      currentRule = formatRule(savedRule);
    }

    const transactions = await accountingService.prisma.bankTransaction.findMany({
      where: { organizationId },
      include: {
        category: { select: { id: true, code: true, description: true, statementLine: true } },
        receipt: true,
        source: { select: { id: true, name: true, type: true } },
        journalEntry: true,
      },
      orderBy: { date: 'desc' },
      take: 500,
    });

    const affectedTransactions = transactions.filter((transaction) => {
      const sourceType = String(transaction?.source?.type || '').toUpperCase();
      const amountDirection = Number(transaction?.amount || 0) > 0 ? 'INCOME' : Number(transaction?.amount || 0) < 0 ? 'EXPENSE' : 'ANY';
      if (currentRule?.meta?.sourceType && sourceType && currentRule.meta.sourceType !== sourceType) return false;
      if (currentRule?.meta?.amountDirection && currentRule.meta.amountDirection !== 'ANY' && currentRule.meta.amountDirection !== amountDirection) return false;
      return matchesPatterns(transaction.description, currentRule.meta || {}, currentRule.name);
    });

    const prompt = String(req.body?.prompt || '').trim() || `Analyze this bookkeeping rule, review its affected transactions, and suggest any edits. Think through the journal entry, not just category and tax.`;
    const suggestedDraft = await AccountingLLMService.draftRuleFromPrompt({
      organizationId,
      userId,
      sessionId,
      prompt,
      existingRule: currentRule,
      selectedTransactions: affectedTransactions.slice(0, sampleLimit),
    });

    return res.json({
      analysis: {
        affectedCount: affectedTransactions.length,
        sampleTransactions: affectedTransactions.slice(0, sampleLimit).map((transaction) => ({
          id: transaction.id,
          date: transaction.date,
          description: transaction.description,
          amount: transaction.amount,
          currentCategory: transaction.category ? `${transaction.category.code} — ${transaction.category.description}` : null,
          hasJournalEntry: Boolean(transaction.journalEntry),
        })),
        currentRule,
        suggestedDraft,
      },
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('AI Rule Analyze Error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to analyze rule.' });
  }
});

router.post('/generate-sql', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { prompt, existingSql } = req.body;
    const { organizationId, id: userId } = req.user;
    const sessionId = req.sessionID || req.sessionId || null;

    if (!prompt) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Prompt is required.' });
    }

    const sql = await AccountingLLMService.generateRuleSQL({
      prompt,
      existingSql,
      organizationId,
      userId,
      sessionId,
    });

    return res.json({ sql });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('AI SQL Generation Error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to generate SQL.' });
  }
});

router.post('/merge', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleIds, name } = req.body;
    const { organizationId } = req.user;

    if (!Array.isArray(ruleIds) || ruleIds.length < 2) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'At least two rule IDs are required.' });
    }
    if (!name || !String(name).trim()) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'A name for the new rule is required.' });
    }

    await accountingService.prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      const rulesToMerge = await tx.accountingRule.findMany({
        where: {
          id: { in: ruleIds },
          organizationId,
        },
        include: { category: true },
      });

      if (rulesToMerge.length !== ruleIds.length) {
        throw new Error('One or more rules were not found.');
      }

      const combinedSql = rulesToMerge
        .map((rule) => `-- Rule: ${rule.name}\n${rule.sql || ''}`)
        .filter((sql) => (sql.split('\n')[1] || '').trim())
        .join('\n\n');

      const combinedMeta = rulesToMerge.map((rule) => parseRuleMeta(rule.patterns));
      const mergedPatterns = normalizePatternList(combinedMeta.flatMap((meta) => meta.patterns));

      const mergedRule = await tx.accountingRule.create({
        data: {
          organizationId,
          name: String(name).trim(),
          sql: combinedSql,
          patterns: serializeRuleMeta({
            patterns: mergedPatterns,
            group: combinedMeta[0]?.group || 'General',
            notes: combinedMeta.map((meta) => meta.notes).filter(Boolean).join('\n\n'),
            enabled: combinedMeta.some((meta) => meta.enabled !== false),
            matchMode: combinedMeta.some((meta) => meta.matchMode === 'ALL') ? 'ALL' : 'ANY',
            sourceType: combinedMeta.find((meta) => meta.sourceType)?.sourceType || null,
            transactionType: combinedMeta.find((meta) => meta.transactionType)?.transactionType || null,
            amountDirection: combinedMeta.find((meta) => meta.amountDirection && meta.amountDirection !== 'ANY')?.amountDirection || 'ANY',
            llmSummary: 'Merged from existing accounting rules.',
            proposalSource: 'MERGE',
          }),
          autoCategory: rulesToMerge.some((rule) => rule.autoCategory),
          autoApplyTaxes: rulesToMerge.some((rule) => rule.autoApplyTaxes),
          requiredReceipt: rulesToMerge.some((rule) => rule.requiredReceipt),
          categoryId: rulesToMerge.find((rule) => rule.categoryId)?.categoryId || null,
        },
      });

      await tx.accountingRule.deleteMany({ where: { id: { in: ruleIds } } });
      return mergedRule;
    });

    return res.json({ message: 'Rules merged successfully.' });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Merge Rules Error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to merge rules.' });
  }
});



router.post('/batch', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const rawRules = Array.isArray(req.body?.rules) ? req.body.rules : [];

    if (!rawRules.length) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'At least one rule is required.' });
    }

    const createdRules = await accountingService.prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
      const created = [];
      for (const rawRule of rawRules) {
        const data = buildRuleDataFromBody(rawRule || {});
        if (!data.name) {
          throw new Error('Every batch rule needs a name.');
        }
        const rule = await tx.accountingRule.create({
          data: { ...data, organizationId },
          include: { category: true },
        });
        created.push(rule);
      }
      return created;
    });

    return res.status(200).json({
      message: `${createdRules.length} rule${createdRules.length === 1 ? '' : 's'} created.`,
      count: createdRules.length,
      rules: createdRules.map(formatRule),
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Batch create rules error:', err);
    return res.status(400).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to create batch rules.' });
  }
});

router.delete('/', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const result = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.deleteMany({ where: { organizationId } });
    return res.json({ message: `Deleted ${result.count} rule${result.count === 1 ? '' : 's'}.`, count: result.count });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Delete all rules error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to delete all rules.' });
  }
});

    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const rules = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findMany({
      where: { organizationId },
      include: { category: true },
      orderBy: [{ updatedAt: 'desc' }, { createdAt: 'desc' }],
    });
    return res.json({ rules: rules.map(formatRule) });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Get rules error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

router.post('/', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const data = buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {});
    if (!data.name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Rule name is required.' });
    }

    const newRule = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.create({
      data: { ...data, organizationId },
      include: { category: true },
    });
    return res.status(200).json({ message: 'Rule created.', rule: formatRule(newRule) });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Create rule error:', err);
    return res.status(400).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

router.put('/:ruleId', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleId } = req.params;
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;

    const existing = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: ruleId }, include: { category: true } });
    if (!existing || existing.organizationId !== organizationId) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found or you do not own it.' });
    }

    const updated = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.update({
      where: { id: ruleId },
      data: buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {}, existing),
      include: { category: true },
    });
    return res.json({ message: 'Rule updated.', rule: formatRule(updated) });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Update rule error:', err);
    return res.status(400).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

router.delete('/:ruleId', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleId } = req.params;
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const existing = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: ruleId } });
    if (!existing || existing.organizationId !== organizationId) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found or you do not own it.' });
    }
    await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.delete({ where: { id: ruleId } });
    return res.json({ message: 'Rule deleted.' });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Delete rule error:', err);
    return res.status(400).json({ error: err.message });
  }
});

router.post('/:ruleId/run', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  const { ruleId } = req.params;
  const { dateFrom, dateTo, transactionIds } = req.body || {};
  const orgId = req.user?.organizationId;
  const prisma = accountingService.prisma;

  try {
    const result = await withSqliteMutex(async () => {
      try {
        await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;');
        await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe('PRAGMA busy_timeout=60000;');
      } catch (_) {}

      const baseWhere = { ...(orgId ? { organizationId: orgId } : {}) };
      if (Array.isArray(transactionIds) && transactionIds.length > 0) {
        const validIds = transactionIds.map((value) => Number(value)).filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value));
        if (validIds.length > 0) {
          baseWhere.id = { in: validIds };
        }
      } else {
        if (dateFrom) baseWhere.date = { ...(baseWhere.date || {}), gte: new Date(dateFrom) };
        if (dateTo) baseWhere.date = { ...(baseWhere.date || {}), lte: new Date(dateTo) };
      }

      const baseTransactions = await prisma.bankTransaction.findMany({
        select: { id: true, description: true, amount: true, sourceId: true, source: { select: { type: true } } },
        where: baseWhere,
      });
      if (!baseTransactions.length) {
        return { message: 'No matching transactions for this selection/period.', matchedAndUpdated: 0, sqlAffected: 0 };
      }

      let rules = [];
      if (ruleId === '__ALL__') {
        rules = await prisma.accountingRule.findMany({
          where: { ...(orgId ? { organizationId: orgId } : {}) },
          include: { category: true },
          orderBy: { createdAt: 'asc' },
        });
        if (!rules.length) return { error: 'No rules found.' };
      } else {
        const rule = await prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: String(ruleId) }, include: { category: true } });
        if (!rule) return { error: 'Rule not found' };
        rules = [rule];
      }

      const CHUNK = 300;
      let totalSqlAffected = 0;
      let totalMetadataAffected = 0;
      const allAffectedIds = new Set();
      const io = getIO();

      for (const [index, rawRule] of rules.entries()) {
        const rule = formatRule(rawRule);
        const meta = rule.meta;
        if (meta.enabled === false) {
          if (rules.length > 1) {
            io.to(orgId).emit('accounting:rule-progress', { message: `Skipping disabled rule: ${rule.name}`, current: index + 1, total: rules.length });
          }
          continue;
        }

        const idsToUpdate = Array.isArray(transactionIds) && transactionIds.length > 0
          ? baseTransactions.map((tx) => tx.id)
          : baseTransactions
              .filter((transaction) => {
                const sourceType = String(transaction?.source?.type || '').toUpperCase();
                const amountDirection = Number(transaction.amount || 0) > 0 ? 'INCOME' : Number(transaction.amount || 0) < 0 ? 'EXPENSE' : 'ANY';
                if (meta.sourceType && sourceType && meta.sourceType !== sourceType) return false;
                if (meta.amountDirection && meta.amountDirection !== 'ANY' && meta.amountDirection !== amountDirection) return false;
                return matchesPatterns(transaction.description, meta, rule.name);
              })
              .map((transaction) => transaction.id);

        if (!idsToUpdate.length) {
          if (rules.length > 1) {
            io.to(orgId).emit('accounting:rule-progress', { message: `Skipping: ${rule.name}`, current: index + 1, total: rules.length });
          }
          if (RULE_DELAY_MS > 0) await sleep(RULE_DELAY_MS);
          continue;
        }

        for (const id of idsToUpdate) allAffectedIds.add(id);

        totalMetadataAffected += await applyRuleMetadataUpdates(prisma, rule, idsToUpdate);

        if (rule.sql && rule.sql.trim()) {
          let attempt = 0;
          while (true) {
            attempt += 1;
            try {
              await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe('BEGIN IMMEDIATE;');
              for (let i = 0; i < idsToUpdate.length; i += CHUNK) {
                const slice = idsToUpdate.slice(i, i + CHUNK);
                const scoped = scopeSqlToIds(rule.sql, slice);
                if (!scoped) continue;
                const affected = await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe(scoped);
                totalSqlAffected += Number(affected) || 0;
              }
              await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe('COMMIT;');
              break;
            } catch (err) {
              try { await prisma.$executeRawUnsafe('ROLLBACK;'); } catch (_) {}
              if (attempt <= 5 && isLockOrTimeout(err)) {
                await sleep(500 * attempt);
                continue;
              }
              throw err;
            }
          }
        }

        for (const transactionId of idsToUpdate) {
          try {
            await accountingService.syncJournalWithBankTx(transactionId, req.user?.id || req.user?.userId || null);
          } catch (syncError) {
            console.warn(`Rule journal sync skipped for transaction ${transactionId}: ${syncError.message}`);
          }
        }

        if (rules.length > 1) {
          io.to(orgId).emit('accounting:rule-progress', { message: `Completed: ${rule.name}`, current: index + 1, total: rules.length });
        }
        if (RULE_DELAY_MS > 0) await sleep(RULE_DELAY_MS);
      }

      const parts = [];
      if (totalMetadataAffected > 0) parts.push(`${totalMetadataAffected} metadata updates`);
      if (totalSqlAffected > 0) parts.push(`${totalSqlAffected} rows affected via SQL`);
      const message = parts.length ? `Rules executed: ${parts.join(', ')}.` : 'Rules executed, no rows affected.';
      return {
        message,
        sqlAffected: totalSqlAffected,
        metadataAffected: totalMetadataAffected,
        affectedTransactionIds: [...allAffectedIds],
      };
    });

    if (result?.error) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: result.error });
    }
    const affectedTransactionIds = Array.isArray(result?.affectedTransactionIds) ? result.affectedTransactionIds : [];
    if (affectedTransactionIds.length) {
      try {
        await TransactionGroupService.refreshAffectedGroups(orgId, affectedTransactionIds);
      } catch (refreshError) {
        console.warn(`Transaction group refresh skipped after rule run: ${refreshError.message}`);
      }
    }
    const { affectedTransactionIds: _affectedTransactionIds, ...responseBody } = result || {};
    return res.json(responseBody);
  } catch (err) {
    if (String(err?.message || '').includes('SQLITE_MUTEX_TIMEOUT') || isLockOrTimeout(err)) {
      return res.status(503).json({ error: 'Database is busy. Please try again in a moment.' });
    }
    console.error(err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: 'Failed to run rule(s).' });
  }
});

router.post('/:ruleId/preview', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleId } = req.params;
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;

    let ruleData;
    if (ruleId === '__DRAFT__') {
      ruleData = buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {});
    } else {
      const existing = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: ruleId } });
      if (!existing || existing.organizationId !== organizationId) {
        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found.' });
      }
      ruleData = { ...existing, ...buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {}, existing) };
    }

    const preview = await RuleSuggestionService.previewRuleImpact(organizationId, ruleData);
    return res.json({ preview });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Rule preview error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to preview rule.' });
  }
});

router.get('/:ruleId/transactions', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleId } = req.params;
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const page = Math.max(1, Number(req.query.page) || 1);
    const pageSize = Math.min(5000, Math.max(1, Number(req.query.pageSize) || 20));
    const filter = String(req.query.filter || 'all').trim();

    const existing = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: ruleId } });
    if (!existing || existing.organizationId !== organizationId) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found.' });
    }

    const meta = parseRuleMeta(existing.patterns);
    const patterns = getEffectivePatterns(meta, existing.name);
    const matchMode = meta.matchMode || 'ANY';
    const sourceType = meta.sourceType || null;
    const amountDirection = meta.amountDirection || 'ANY';

    const allTransactions = await accountingService.prisma.bankTransaction.findMany({
      where: { organizationId },
      include: {
        category: { select: { id: true, code: true, description: true } },
        source: { select: { id: true, name: true, type: true } },
        journalEntry: { select: { id: true, posted: true } },
        receipt: { select: { id: true } },
      },
      orderBy: { date: 'desc' },
    });

    const matched = allTransactions.filter((tx) => {
      const direction = Number(tx.amount || 0) > 0 ? 'INCOME' : 'EXPENSE';
      if (amountDirection !== 'ANY' && amountDirection !== direction) return false;
      if (sourceType) {
        const txSource = String(tx.source?.type || '').toUpperCase();
        if (txSource && txSource !== sourceType) return false;
      }
      if (!patterns.length) return false;
      const haystack = normalizeText(tx.description);
      const normalizedPatterns = patterns.map((p) => normalizeText(p));
      return matchMode === 'ALL'
        ? normalizedPatterns.every((p) => haystack.includes(p))
        : normalizedPatterns.some((p) => haystack.includes(p));
    });

    const filtered = matched.filter((tx) => {
      if (filter === 'all') return true;
      const noCategory = !tx.categoryId;
      const noTax = transactionNeedsTax(tx, meta);
      const noReceipt = existing.requiredReceipt && !tx.receipt;
      const unposted = !tx.journalEntry?.id || tx.journalEntry.posted !== true;
      if (filter === 'incomplete') return noCategory || noTax || noReceipt || unposted;
      if (filter === 'noCategory') return noCategory;
      if (filter === 'noTax') return noTax;
      if (filter === 'noReceipt') return noReceipt;
      if (filter === 'unposted') return unposted;
      return true;
    });

    const summarize = (rows) => {
      const noCategory = rows.filter((tx) => !tx.categoryId);
      const noTax = rows.filter((tx) => transactionNeedsTax(tx, meta));
      const noReceipt = existing.requiredReceipt ? rows.filter((tx) => !tx.receipt) : [];
      const unposted = rows.filter((tx) => !tx.journalEntry?.id || tx.journalEntry.posted !== true);
      const incompleteIds = new Set([
        ...noCategory.map((tx) => tx.id),
        ...noTax.map((tx) => tx.id),
        ...noReceipt.map((tx) => tx.id),
        ...unposted.map((tx) => tx.id),
      ]);

      return {
        totalCount: rows.length,
        totalAmount: Number(rows.reduce((sum, tx) => sum + Math.abs(Number(tx.amount || 0)), 0).toFixed(2)),
        totalTax: Number(rows.reduce((sum, tx) => sum + Math.abs(Number(tx.taxes || 0)), 0).toFixed(2)),
        categorizedCount: rows.length - noCategory.length,
        uncategorizedCount: noCategory.length,
        missingTaxCount: noTax.length,
        missingReceiptCount: noReceipt.length,
        unpostedCount: unposted.length,
        incompleteCount: incompleteIds.size,
      };
    };

    const summary = summarize(matched);
    const filteredSummary = summarize(filtered);
    const totalCount = filtered.length;

    const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;
    const pagedTransactions = filtered.slice(offset, offset + pageSize).map((tx) => {
      const storedProposal = accountingService.getAIProposalFromNotes(tx.notes) || {};
      const taxTreatment = meta.taxTreatment || storedProposal?.likelyTaxTreatment || null;

      return {
        id: tx.id,
        date: tx.date,
        description: tx.description,
        amount: tx.amount,
        taxes: tx.taxes,
        taxTreatment,
        sourceType: tx.source?.type || null,
        sourceName: tx.source?.name || null,
        categoryId: tx.categoryId,
        currentCategoryCode: tx.category?.code || null,
        currentCategoryDescription: tx.category?.description || null,
        hasJournalEntry: Boolean(tx.journalEntry?.id && tx.journalEntry.posted === true),
        hasReceipt: Boolean(tx.receipt),
        receiptRequired: Boolean(existing.requiredReceipt),
        postingType: tx.postingType,
        needsTax: transactionNeedsTax(tx, meta),
      };
    });

    return res.json({
      transactions: pagedTransactions,
      ...summary,
      filteredCount: totalCount,
      filteredSummary,
      page,
      pageSize,
      totalPages: Math.ceil(totalCount / pageSize),
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Get rule transactions error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to get rule transactions.' });
  }
});

// POST /rules/:ruleId/coverage-preview/:groupId — coverage preview against a specific group
router.post('/:ruleId/coverage-preview/:groupId', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const { ruleId, groupId } = req.params;
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;

    let ruleData;
    if (ruleId === '__DRAFT__') {
      ruleData = buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {});
    } else {
      const existing = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findUnique({ where: { id: ruleId } });
      if (!existing || existing.organizationId !== organizationId) {
        return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Rule not found.' });
      }
      ruleData = buildRuleDataFromBody(req.body || {}, existing);
    }

    const preview = await TransactionGroupService.getRuleCoveragePreview(organizationId, ruleData, groupId);
    return res.json({ preview });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Rule coverage preview error:', err);
    const status = err.statusCode || 500;
    return res.status(status).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to get coverage preview.' });
  }
});

router.get('/conflicts', authenticate, async (req, res) => {
  try {
    const organizationId = req.user.organizationId;
    const rules = await accountingService.prisma.accountingRule.findMany({
      where: { organizationId, status: { in: ['ACTIVE', 'DRAFT'] } },
      include: { category: true },
    });

    const conflictPairs = [];
    const normalizeStr = (v) =>
      String(v || '').toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9\s]/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();

    for (let i = 0; i < rules.length; i++) {
      for (let j = i + 1; j < rules.length; j++) {
        const rA = rules[i];
        const rB = rules[j];
        const metaA = parseRuleMeta(rA.patterns);
        const metaB = parseRuleMeta(rB.patterns);
        const patternsA = metaA.patterns.map(normalizeStr);
        const patternsB = metaB.patterns.map(normalizeStr);
        const overlap = patternsA.filter((p) => patternsB.includes(p));
        if (overlap.length && rA.categoryId && rB.categoryId && rA.categoryId !== rB.categoryId) {
          conflictPairs.push({
            ruleA: { id: rA.id, name: rA.name, category: rA.category ? `${rA.category.code} — ${rA.category.description}` : null },
            ruleB: { id: rB.id, name: rB.name, category: rB.category ? `${rB.category.code} — ${rB.category.description}` : null },
            overlappingPatterns: overlap,
            conflictType: 'DIFFERENT_CATEGORY',
          });
        }
      }
    }

    return res.json({ conflicts: conflictPairs, count: conflictPairs.length });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Conflict detection error:', err);
    return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message || 'Failed to detect conflicts.' });
  }
});

export default router;

```
